macro_markets.query: What are the most important macroeconomic and stock market news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major index movements, Fed/central bank news, and significant company earnings or announcements.

crypto_news.query: What are the most significant cryptocurrency and blockchain news from the past 24 hours? Include major price movements, regulatory news, and notable project developments.

items.title: Bitcoin Surges Past $76,000 Amid US-Iran Ceasefire Negotiations
items.summary: Bitcoin prices broke through $76,000, boosting total crypto market cap to $2.55 trillion by over 1%, ahead of US-Iran talks in Islamabad. The rally triggered $217 million in liquidations, mostly shorts at $140 million, with analysts eyeing $80,000 by month-end.[2][4]

items.title: XRP Hits $1.43 with Bullish MACD Flip and Whale Accumulation
items.summary: XRP traded at $1.43, up 1.33% in 24 hours and outperforming BTC and ETH weekly, as daily MACD turned bullish for the first time since January 2026. Whales accumulated 360 million XRP, with exchange reserves dropping to 2.76 billion tokens signaling reduced sell pressure.[3]

items.title: Kelp DAO Suffers $290M rsETH Hack, Triggering DeFi Crisis and Fund Freezes
items.summary: Kelp DAO lost $290M in rsETH via Layer Zero exploit, pushing multiple lending markets to full utilization in DeFi crisis mode. Arbitrum froze $71M in linked ETH, while Aave and others address frozen WETH reserves on multiple networks.[1][4]

items.title: StarkWare Lays Off Staff After Starknet Revenue Drops 98%
items.summary: StarkWare fired staff following a 98% collapse in Starknet revenue, highlighting challenges in the layer-2 ecosystem.

items.title: RAVE Token Crashes 97% Prompting Investigations by Binance, Bitget, Gate
items.summary: Exchanges Binance, Bitget, and Gate launched probes into RAVE token after its 97% plunge, amid rising scam concerns.

items.title: Bitcoin Dips Below $75,000 in Later Session, Down 1.67%
items.summary: Bitcoin fell below $75,000 with a 1.67% 24-hour decline, shifting short-term momentum to bearish as sellers regained control on elevated volume.[5]

ai_news.query: What are the most important artificial intelligence and machine learning news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include new model releases, company announcements, research breakthroughs, and industry trends.

items.title: GPUBreach Research Disclosed: New Hardware-Level GPU Vulnerability Reshapes Security Threat Model
items.summary: Researchers at the University of Toronto publicly disclosed GPUBreach, a critical vulnerability affecting GPUs with GDDR6 memory that evolves the Rowhammer technique. The research signals a paradigm shift in threat modeling for AI infrastructure and multi-tenant cloud platforms, with code artifacts scheduled to release on April 17.

items.title: Language Models Generate Novel Quantum Experiments in Scientific Breakthrough
items.summary: Researchers led by Sören Arlt at the University of Tübingen developed a language model capable of generating blueprints for quantum optics setups, published in Nature Machine Intelligence. The system successfully designed experimental configurations for desired quantum states, potentially accelerating research in quantum computing and quantum communication.

items.title: NSA Adopts Anthropic's Mythos Model Despite Supply Chain Risk Designation
items.summary: The National Security Agency is using Anthropic's Mythos model, a powerful AI system capable of finding zero-day vulnerabilities and orchestrating autonomous attacks, despite the U.S. government recently designating Anthropic a 'supply chain risk' over ethical concerns regarding military use.

items.title: Anthropic Faces Compute Crunch, Caps Token Consumption and Raises Inference Costs
items.summary: Anthropic has implemented token consumption caps during peak hours across certain pricing tiers and released Claude Opus 4.7, which consumes more tokens and costs more per query than previous versions, reflecting ongoing AI infrastructure capacity constraints.

items.title: CPUID Supply Chain Attack Compromises CPU-Z and HWMonitor Downloads
items.summary: The CPUID website hosting popular hardware monitoring tools was compromised in a supply chain attack lasting less than 24 hours between April 9-10, with attackers redirecting download links to malicious executables through a compromised secondary API.

gaming.query: What are the most notable video game and gaming industry news from the past 24 hours? Include game releases, announcements, esports, and industry developments.

items.title: Nioh 3 Announced with PS5 Demo Available Now
items.summary: Team Ninja and Koei Tecmo announced Nioh 3 during June's PlayStation State of Play, allowing soulslike fans to play a demo immediately on PS5. The game is slated for a 2026 release.

items.title: Bloodstained Sequel 'The Scarlet Engagement' Revealed at State of Play
items.summary: ArtPlay unveiled The Scarlet Engagement, a new Bloodstained game differing significantly from Ritual of the Night, during the June PlayStation State of Play showcase.

items.title: Final Fantasy Tactics - The Ivalice Chronicles Set for September Launch
items.summary: A remaster of Final Fantasy Tactics titled The Ivalice Chronicles is launching in September, revealed among major franchise updates at June 2025's State of Play.

items.title: Xbox Game Pass Confirms June 2025 Wave 1 Lineup
items.summary: Microsoft revealed the full list of games joining Xbox Game Pass in the first wave of June 2025, ahead of the Xbox Games Showcase.

items.title: Silent Hill f Release Date Advanced, Coming Sooner Than Expected
items.summary: Silent Hill f received a release date sooner than anticipated during the June PlayStation State of Play, alongside other major reveals.

items.title: No More Heroes Developer Announces New Ultraviolent Sci-Fi Game
items.summary: Suda51 and Grasshopper Manufacture revealed a new over-the-top action game blending No More Heroes and Dead Rising vibes.

items.title: Helldivers 2 Unveils 'Force of Law' Warbond
items.summary: Helldivers 2 announced the new Force of Law Warbond, available soon for players in the cooperative shooter.

items.title: Dragon Ball Project: Multi Name and Network Test Dates Revealed
items.summary: The real name of Dragon Ball Project: Multi was disclosed along with dates for a new network test.

items.title: Classic Stealth Franchise Revived for VR at State of Play
items.summary: Maze Theory and Vertigo Games announced a VR return for a dormant stealth franchise during June's State of Play.

items.title: Splatoon Raiders New Trailer Showcases Treasure-Hunting Adventure
items.summary: Nintendo released a new trailer for Splatoon Raiders, highlighting character creation, gadgets, and its single-player adventure.

tech_news.query: What are the most important technology news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major product launches, company news, and significant tech industry developments. Exclude AI-specific news.

items.title: Tim Cook Steps Down as Apple CEO After 15 Years; John Ternus to Take Over
items.summary: After 15 years leading Apple, Tim Cook is stepping down to make way for insider John Ternus, marking a significant leadership transition at the tech giant.

items.title: NASA's Artemis II Crew Returns Stunning Moon Photos Including Earthrise
items.summary: Astronauts from the Artemis II mission have sent back remarkable photographs from the Moon, including views of Earthrise and meteoroid flashes, marking progress in lunar exploration.

items.title: Australia's Syenta Raises $26 Million to Address AI Chip Bottleneck
items.summary: Syenta, an Australian startup, secured $26 million in funding with former Intel CEO joining its board to help alleviate semiconductor supply constraints.

items.title: Meta Installs Tracking Software on US Employee Computers
items.summary: Meta has deployed new tracking software on U.S.-based employees' computers to monitor mouse movements, clicks, and keystrokes in work-related applications.

items.title: HCL Tech Reports Strong Q4 Results with 12% Revenue Growth
items.summary: HCL Technologies posted Q4 results showing 12% revenue increase and declared a Rs 24 per share dividend.

items.title: Persistent Systems Q4 Profit Jumps 34% Year-over-Year
items.summary: Persistent Systems reported a 34% year-over-year profit increase in Q4 with revenue up 25%, reaching Rs 529 crore.
